records.ety: [OE. chek, OF. eschec, F. chec, a stop, hindrance, orig. check in the game of chess, pl. checs chess, through Ar., fr. Pers. shāh king. See Shah, and cf. Checkmate, Chess, Checker.]

defs: A word of warning denoting that the king is in danger; such a menace of a player's king by an adversary's move as would, if it were any other piece, expose it to immediate capture. A king so menaced is said to be in check, and must be made safe at the next move.
A condition of interrupted or impeded progress; arrest; stop; delay; as, to hold an enemy in check.
quotes: Which gave a remarkable check to the first progress of Christianity.
No check, no stay, this streamlet fears.

defs: Whatever arrests progress, or limits action; an obstacle, guard, restraint, or rebuff.
quotes: Useful check upon the administration of government.
A man whom no check could abash.

defs: A mark, certificate, or token, by which, errors may be prevented, or a thing or person may be identified; as, checks placed against items in an account; a check given for baggage; a return check on a railroad.
A written order directing a bank or banker to pay money as therein stated. See Bank check, below.
A woven or painted design in squares resembling the pattern of a checkerboard; one of the squares of such a design; also, cloth having such a figure.
The forsaking by a hawk of its proper game to follow other birds.
Small chick or crack.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Checked (chkt); p. pr. & vb. n. checking.]

defs: To make a move which puts an adversary's piece, esp. his king, in check; to put in check.
To put a sudden restraint upon; to stop temporarily; to hinder; to repress; to curb.
To obstruct the motion of an opposing player by contacting him with one's body.
quotes: So many clogs to check and retard the headlong course of violence and oppression.

defs: To verify, to guard, to make secure, by means of a mark, token, or other check; to distinguish by a check; to put a mark against (an item) after comparing with an original or a counterpart in order to secure accuracy.
To compare with an original or with some standard, to insure correctness; as, to check an account; to check with a supervisor about procedures.
To chide, rebuke, or reprove.
quotes: The good king, his master, will check him for it.

defs: To slack or ease off, as a brace which is too stiffly extended.
To make checks or chinks in; to cause to crack; as, the sun checks timber.
To leave (something) in the temporary custody of another; as, to check baggage; to check one's firearms at the door; to check one's coat at the cloakroom.
To accept (something) for temporary custody from another; as, to check a customer's baggage; to check a customer's coat.
To make a checkered pattern upon.
To clash or interfere.
To act as a curb or restraint.
quotes: It [his presence] checks too strong upon me.

defs: To crack or gape open, as wood in drying; or to crack in small checks, as varnish, paint, etc.
To turn, when in pursuit of proper game, and fly after other birds.
quotes: And like the haggard, check at every feather That comes before his eye.

defs: Checkered; designed in checks.
